articles.headline: Harry Kane vs. Erling Haaland: How do England, Norway stars compare? Key stats before World Cup clash
articles.description: Who will come out on top in the World Cup quarterfinal, Harry Kane or Erling Haaland? Here is what the stats say.

images.name: Harry Kane Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.alt: An image of Harry Kane alongside one of Erling Haaland.
images.credit: Photo by Luis Cano/Jam Media/Getty Images and Photo by Richard Sellers/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images

images.name: Erling Haaland [1296x729]
images.alt: Erling Haaland wears a viking helmet.
images.credit: (Photo by Julian Finney - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images)

images.name: Harry Kane penalty [1296x729]
images.alt: Harry Kane takes the penalty against Mexico.

images.name: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'
images.caption: Odegaard: Norway's quarterfinal against England a 'massive test'

articles.headline: Police officer injured, arrests made amid London disorder after France beat Morocco
articles.description: A police officer was injured and four people were arrested after disorder broke out in London following France's 2-0 win against Morocco in the World Cup quarterfinal on Thursday.

images.name: Metropolitan Police stock [1296x729]
images.alt: The logo of the Metropolitan Police can be seen on a police car in central London.
images.credit: Anna Ross/picture alliance via Getty Images

articles.headline: Instead of its biggest World Cup star, Pulisic was U.S.'s biggest mystery
articles.description: In his prime at a World Cup on home soil, this summer was Christian Pulisic's moment, but for a number of reasons, he failed to meet it.

articles.headline: World Cup quarterfinals: How we got here, predictions, odds
articles.description: Four weeks after the World Cup began, the quarterfinals are underway, with each team three wins from lifting the trophy. Next up, Spain vs. Belgium.

images.caption: Burley: Spain need more than Yamal to win World Cup

article.headline: Raúl Jiménez, Julián Quiñones help Mexico past Ecuador, into Round of 16
article.description: Mexico will now face either England or DR Congo in Mexico City in the Round of 16.
article.linkText: Jiménez, Quiñones help Mexico past Ecuador, into R16

images.name: Raul Jiimenez Julian Quinones Celebration - Mexico vs Ecuador - FIFA World Cup 2026 [608x342]

peers.name: Raul Jiimenez Julian Quinones Celebration - Mexico vs Ecuador - FIFA World Cup 2026 [1296x1296]
Raul Jiimenez Julian Quinones Celebration - Mexico vs Ecuador - FIFA World Cup 2026 [1296x729]
Raul Jiimenez Julian Quinones Celebration - Mexico vs Ecuador - FIFA World Cup 2026 [864x1296]
Raul Jiimenez Julian Quinones Celebration - Mexico vs Ecuador - FIFA World Cup 2026 [1296x864]
Raul Jiimenez Julian Quinones Celebration - Mexico vs Ecuador - FIFA World Cup 2026 [1296x518]

images.name: Mexico boss: WC knockout win vs. Ecuador a 'perfect night'
images.caption: Mexico boss: WC knockout win vs. Ecuador a 'perfect night'

article.story:
A daunting trip to the Azteca Stadium awaits England if they beat DR Congo after co-hosts Mexico brushed aside Ecuador 2-0 in their last-32 World Cup tie.

Kick-off in Mexico City was delayed by an hour due to a thunderstorm, but that did not stop the hosts from making an electrifying start in front of more than 80,000 raucous fans.

Gilberto Mora fired narrowly over from distance and Raúl Jiménez headed a fraction wide inside the first 10 minutes, while John Yeboah hit the post for Ecuador against the run of play before Mexico went ahead midway through the first half.

Julián Quiñones was sent clear down the left by Roberto Alvarado and cut inside before firing into the top corner.

Another fine finish doubled Mexico's advantage in the 31st minute, Jimenez linking up with Quinones before smashing his shot into the roof of the net from the edge of the area.

Yeboah was denied by Raúl Rangel before half-time as Ecuador sought a route back into the game.

Ecuador keeper Hernán Galíndez made an excellent save after 66 minutes to keep out a César Montes header from a corner, while at the other end, Kevin Rodríguez prodded wide when played in by Moisés Caicedo, but the South American side were unable to mount any sort of comeback.

A miserable evening for Ecuador was compounded by Arsenal defender Piero Hincapié being sent off in stoppage time for covering his mouth during an altercation with Santiago Gimenez.

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.name: aerialsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels where the ball is in the air and possession is lost

stats.name: aerialsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where the ball is in the air and possession is won

stats.name: duelsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.name: duels
stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelWinPct
stats.description: The percentage of duels won.

stats.name: turnover
stats.description: An unsuccessful touch. A player only touched the ball and lost possession – bad control

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.name: touches
stats.description: Total sum of a player's or team's on-the-ball events

stats.name: aerialDuelPct
stats.description: Percentage of aerial duels won against total contested duels.
